hook: Clean up messy commits before pushing
code_skeleton:
git rebase -i HEAD~3
# In editor:
# pick abc1234 Add feature
# squash def5678 Fix typo
# drop ghi9012 WIP commit

hook: Find the exact commit that broke everything
code_skeleton:
git bisect start
git bisect bad          # current is broken
git bisect good v1.0    # this version worked
# Git checks out middle commit
# Test it, then: git bisect good/bad
# Repeat until found!

hook: Save work in progress with context
code_skeleton:
git stash push -m 'WIP: auth refactor'
git stash push -m 'WIP: new API endpoint'

git stash list
# stash@{0}: WIP: new API endpoint
# stash@{1}: WIP: auth refactor

git stash pop stash@{1}  # restore specific stash

git worktree add ../hotfix hotfix-branch
# Now you have two working directories!
# Main repo on 'main', ../hotfix on 'hotfix-branch'

cd ../hotfix
# Make fixes here without stashing main work

git worktree remove ../hotfix  # cleanup

hook: Undo almost anything — even hard resets
code_skeleton:
# Accidentally ran git reset --hard?
git reflog
# abc1234 HEAD@{0}: reset: moving to HEAD~3
# def5678 HEAD@{1}: commit: important work

git reset --hard def5678  # restored!

hook: Grab specific commits from other branches
code_skeleton:
# On main branch, grab one commit from feature:
git cherry-pick abc1234

# Grab a range:
git cherry-pick abc1234..def5678

# Without committing (just stage changes):
git cherry-pick --no-commit abc1234

# Create .git-blame-ignore-revs
echo 'abc1234' >> .git-blame-ignore-revs

# Use it:
git blame --ignore-revs-file .git-blame-ignore-revs file.py

# Set globally:
git config blame.ignoreRevsFile .git-blame-ignore-revs

hook: Share changes without pushing to remote
code_skeleton:
# Create a patch from last 2 commits:
git format-patch -2

# Apply patches:
git am 0001-fix-bug.patch

# Or from unstaged changes:
git diff > changes.patch
git apply changes.patch

hook: Find commits by content, not just message
code_skeleton:
# Search commit CONTENTS for a string:
git log -S 'TODO' --oneline

# Search with regex:
git log -G 'def\s+process' --oneline

# Filter by author + date:
git log --author='alice' --since='2024-01-01' --oneline

git config --global alias.co checkout
git config --global alias.br branch
git config --global alias.st 'status -sb'
git config --global alias.lg 'log --oneline --graph --all'
git config --global alias.undo 'reset HEAD~1 --mixed'

# Usage: git lg, git undo, git st
